mission.title: Install and Configure Dapr on Kubernetes
mission.description: Dapr is a portable runtime for building distributed applications across cloud and edge, combining event-driven architecture with workflow orchestration. Installing Dapr allows you to easily manage microservices and their interactions in a Kubernetes environment.

steps.title: Add the Dapr Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official Dapr Helm repository to your Helm configuration.
```bash
helm repo add dapr https://dapr.github.io/helm-charts
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Dapr using Helm
steps.description:
Install Dapr in the `dapr-system` namespace with the specified version.
```bash
helm install dapr dapr/dapr --namespace dapr-system --create-namespace --version 1.17.0
```

steps.title: Verify Dapr installation
steps.description:
Check the status of the Dapr pods to ensure they are running.
```bash
kubectl get pods -n dapr-system
```

steps.title: Configure Dapr for monitoring
steps.description:
Enable Prometheus monitoring by updating the values for Dapr.
```bash
kubectl patch deployment dapr-operator -n dapr-system --type='json' -p='[{"op": "add", "path": "/spec/template/spec/containers/0/env/-", "value": {"name": "ENABLE_PROMETHEUS", "value": "true"}}]'
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Dapr Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the Dapr release from the cluster.
```bash
helm uninstall dapr --namespace dapr-system
```

uninstall.title: Clean up Dapr resources
uninstall.description:
Delete any remaining resources and Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs).
```bash
kubectl delete crd $(kubectl get crd | grep dapr.io | awk '{print $1}')
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Dapr namespace
uninstall.description:
Delete the namespace used for Dapr.
```bash
kubectl delete namespace dapr-system
```

upgrade.title: Backup current Dapr state
upgrade.description:
Export the current Dapr configuration and resources for backup.
```bash
kubectl get all -n dapr-system -o yaml > dapr-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update the Helm repository
upgrade.description:
Ensure the Dapr Helm repository is up to date.
```bash
helm repo update
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade Dapr installation
upgrade.description:
Upgrade Dapr to the latest version.
```bash
helm upgrade dapr dapr/dapr --namespace dapr-system --version 1.17.0
```

upgrade.title: Verify Dapr upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check the status of the Dapr pods to ensure they are running the new version.
```bash
kubectl get pods -n dapr-system
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in Pending state
troubleshooting.description:
If Dapr pods are stuck in Pending, check for insufficient resources or scheduling issues.
```bash
kubectl describe pod <pod-name> -n dapr-system
```

troubleshooting.title: Dapr sidecar not injecting
troubleshooting.description:
If the Dapr sidecar is not injecting into your application pods, ensure the annotations are set correctly in your deployment.
```bash
kubectl get deployment <your-deployment> -o yaml
```

troubleshooting.title: Dapr API not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
If the Dapr API is not reachable, check the service configuration and ensure the service is running.
```bash
kubectl get svc -n dapr-system
```

troubleshooting.title: Prometheus metrics not showing
troubleshooting.description:
If Prometheus metrics are not showing, verify that the Dapr pods have the monitoring enabled and check the Prometheus configuration.
```bash
kubectl logs <dapr-operator-pod-name> -n dapr-system
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Dapr will have all pods running in the `dapr-system` namespace and the Dapr API should be accessible. You can also verify that monitoring is enabled and functioning correctly.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add dapr https://dapr.github.io/helm-charts
helm repo update

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running and Helm installed.
